chunk_summary: Guidelines for accurate recumbent length and standing height measurements to minimize errors in malnutrition classification, emphasizing repeat rules and proper positioning techniques.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of accurate anthropometric measurements in malnutrition classification.
Learn the correct procedures for measuring recumbent length using an infantometer.
Master the standing height measurement protocol using a stadiometer.
Recognize when to defer length/height measurements and apply alternative triage criteria.
key_facts: Errors in length/height measurements can shift z-scores across critical -2 or -3 SD cutoffs for wasting and stunting.
Recumbent length requires a rigid length board, two trained measurers, and measurements to the nearest 0.1 cm.
Repeat measurements if discrepancies exceed 1 cm; reposition and remeasure until closest agreement is achieved.
Standing height requires a stadiometer, proper positioning (heels together, knees straight, head in Frankfort plane), and rechecks for slouching or bending.
Do not interchange length and height without noting the method; apply consistent adjustments if substitution is necessary.
reference_quote: Length/height is the most common point of failure in nutrition classification because small errors shift both wasting and stunting z-scores across -2 or -3 SD cutoffs. (WHO Child Growth Standards)
document_key_topics: Recumbent length and standing height protocols
Impact of measurement errors on malnutrition classification
Alternative triage criteria for deferred measurements
